title: Alianza Petrolera F.C.

infobox.value: [Estadio Daniel Villa Zapata](/wiki/Estadio_Daniel_Villa_Zapata "Estadio Daniel Villa Zapata")

[Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") [Juan Guillermo Arboleda](/w/index.php?title=Juan_Guillermo_Arboleda&action=edit&redlink=1 "Juan Guillermo Arboleda (page does not exist)") | [MF](/wiki/Midfielder "Midfielder") | 2012, 2013– | **105**

[Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") [Leonardo Torres](/w/index.php?title=Leonardo_Antonio_Torres&action=edit&redlink=1 "Leonardo Antonio Torres (page does not exist)") | [DF](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)") | 2006, 2009–2011 | **69**

was a Colombian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") team based in [Barrancabermeja](/wiki/Barrancabermeja "Barrancabermeja"), [Santander](/wiki/Santander_Department "Santander Department"), that last played in the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A"). The club was founded in 1991 and played in the [Categoría Primera B](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B "Categoría Primera B") until 2012. They played their home games at the [Daniel Villa Zapata](/wiki/Estadio_Daniel_Villa_Zapata "Estadio Daniel Villa Zapata") stadium. They also played home matches in the town of [Guarne](/wiki/Guarne "Guarne"), [Antioquia](/wiki/Antioquia_Department "Antioquia Department"), in [Yopal](/wiki/Yopal "Yopal"), [Casanare](/wiki/Casanare_Department "Casanare Department") and in [Floridablanca](/wiki/Floridablanca,_Santander "Floridablanca, Santander").

Colombian football club

Football club

Alianza Petrolera

**Alianza Petrolera Fútbol Club** was a Colombian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") team based in [Barrancabermeja](/wiki/Barrancabermeja "Barrancabermeja"), [Santander](/wiki/Santander_Department "Santander Department"), that last played in the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A"). The club was founded in 1991 and played in the [Categoría Primera B](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B "Categoría Primera B") until 2012. They played their home games at the [Daniel Villa Zapata](/wiki/Estadio_Daniel_Villa_Zapata "Estadio Daniel Villa Zapata") stadium. They also played home matches in the town of [Guarne](/wiki/Guarne "Guarne"), [Antioquia](/wiki/Antioquia_Department "Antioquia Department"), in [Yopal](/wiki/Yopal "Yopal"), [Casanare](/wiki/Casanare_Department "Casanare Department") and in [Floridablanca](/wiki/Floridablanca,_Santander "Floridablanca, Santander").

The club was dissolved in January 2024, with [Alianza F.C.](/wiki/Alianza_F.C._(Colombia) "Alianza F.C. (Colombia)") taking their place.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alianza_Petrolera_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Alianza Petrolera was founded on 24 October 1990, entering the second-tier competition [Categoría Primera B](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B "Categoría Primera B") where they played from 1992 to 2012. They enjoyed a significant run of success between 1998 and 2004, finishing as runners-up in 2002. However, their fortunes changed after that and by 2009 the club was on the verge of folding.

In the 2009 Apertura, they performed dismally, earning just five points and not winning a single game. The club was short on sponsors, coaches, and players, and their participation in the Finalizacion seemed doubtful. They managed to compete, but finished dead last. In early 2011, Alianza Petrolera signed a partnership deal with [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional") that would net them players and coaching staff on loan, with much of the wages covered by the latter team.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

This new arrangement revitalized the club, which qualified for the finals of the "Torneo Finalización" in 2012. This earned them a berth in the final against [Deportivo Rionegro](/wiki/Itag%C3%BC%C3%AD_Leones_F.C. "Itagüí Leones F.C."). Alianza prevailed, winning the first leg 1–0 and the second leg 3–1. That earned them a spot in the season final against [América de Cali](/wiki/Am%C3%A9rica_de_Cali "América de Cali"), with promotion on the line. The first leg ended 2–1 in favor of Alianza, but the second leg ended 1–0 in favor of América de Cali. The series went to penalty kicks, where Alianza Petrolera prevailed and earned promotion to the top flight for the very first time.

Alianza has not been relegated back since. Their best campaign in the top flight was in the [2015 Finalización](/wiki/2015_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season#Torneo_Finalización "2015 Categoría Primera A season") tournament. During the first stage, the club enjoyed a consistent performance, leading the table for some rounds of the tournament and ending in sixth place with 33 points, which allowed them to qualify for the knockout stages for the first time, losing to [Independiente Medellín](/wiki/Independiente_Medell%C3%ADn "Independiente Medellín") in the quarterfinals.

After finishing in eighth place in the aggregate table of the [2023 Categoría Primera A season](/wiki/2023_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2023 Categoría Primera A season"), Alianza Petrolera qualified for their first international competition, the [2024 Copa Sudamericana](/wiki/2024_Copa_Sudamericana "2024 Copa Sudamericana"). However, on 16 January 2024 the club announced their departure from Barrancabermeja due to the lack of financial support from local authorities, accepting a proposal from the city of [Valledupar](/wiki/Valledupar "Valledupar") to move there. With the move to Valledupar, the club was also rebranded to [Alianza F.C.](/wiki/Alianza_F.C._(Colombia) "Alianza F.C. (Colombia)"), dropping its colors and the word *Petrolera* from their name, and adopting as new colors those representative of their new hometown.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Consequently, its successor club Alianza F.C. inherited the Copa Sudamericana berth earned by Alianza Petrolera.
